AppInfoHelpPage.description:
Desktop Search Engine

Search local files by name and content
uses Python multiprocessing with brute-force search
Design based on internet search engines
Result ranking with or without keyword weighting
Context snippets for content matches.

SettingsHelpPage.ranking_description:
The first search term has a higher weight than the second, etc. Example: Searching for 'John Doe Invoice'
John (3) + Doe (2) + Invoice (1) = 6/6 points = 1.0 (perfect match)
Only 'Doe Invoice' = 2 + 1 = 3/6 = 0.5 (lower ranking).
Only 'Invoice' (1) = 1/6 = rounded to 0.2
If you want invoices to appear higher, you would need to search for 'Invoice John Doe'.

The first search term also always determines the context in the snippet.
SettingsHelpPage.results_description:
The file path is always checked first:
• If a match has a ranking above 0.5, it is immediately displayed as a FILENAME match
• If the ranking is below 0.5 or 0.0, the content search is started

A content match is always displayed - regardless of ranking or priority.
SettingsHelpPage.snippet_description:
Determines how many characters left and right of the match are displayed.
Example: With size 50, 50 characters before and 50 after the match are shown.
SettingsHelpPage.search_depth_description:
Determines how many characters per file are read during content search.
• Average text page has about 2000-3000 characters
• Higher values = more thorough search, but slower
• Lower values = faster search, but possibly incomplete

PDF files always read at least the first page, regardless of setting.
SettingsHelpPage.default_path_description:
The path preselected in the directory dialog when the app starts.
Can be changed via 'Choose Directory'.
The last selected path is remembered for the next session and written to cache.
SettingsHelpPage.language_description: After changing the language, the application must be restarted.
SettingsHelpPage.console_description:
The button next to the progress bar opens the console.
It shows detailed information about the search process,
including file processing, matches found, and performance data.
